titleStmt.title: Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 71

p.seg: Richard, by the grace of God humble servant of the church of St Andrews, to all sons of Holy Mother Church, clerk and lay, Greeting:
Since we know that it is our responsibility to build up the Church of God, and to change for the better those things that have been built,

we have judged worthy to be confirmed those things that belong to the church of Dunfermline, to take them under the tranquillity of our peace and to confirm them by our charter. Instructed and supported by the Lord’s authority, we have notified those absent as well as those present that we have granted, and by our charter made firm, that the aforementioned church should possess the churches and their possessions, and all ecclesiastical benefices that have been bestowed in alms to the same by the generosity of kings, prelates, and other faithful people, as freely and honourably and quit from every service and exaction as any church well and honourably and freely and quietly holds and possesses its possessions in the whole of our bishopric, namely, the church of Perth, and the school of the same toun, the castle chapel, and the church of St Leonard, the church of Friarton, the church of Stirling, the school of the same toun, and castle chapel, and the rest of the things rightfully belonging to them, the church of Kellie, and the church of Crombie, and the church of Newburn, and the church of Kinglassie, and the chapel of Inverkeithing, and the church of Inveresk, and the chapel of Cousland, and the teinds of Keith, the church of Woolmet, and the church of Hailes with all its belongings, and the church of Newton, and the church of West Calder, saving episcopal right and custom.

With these witnesses: Gregory, bishop of Dunkeld; William, abbot of Holyrood; Walter, prior of St Andrews; Matthew, archdeacon; Andrew, archdeacon of Lothian; Aiulfo, dean; John, nephew of Bishop Robert; Roberto, son of Seolf; Master Herbert; Alexander, chaplain; Master Bartholomew; Master Turpin; Master Abraham; Simon, the archdeacon’s chaplain.
